title: adhatoda longifolia
intro: Adhatoda longifolia, also known as Vasa or Malabar nut, is a medicinal plant species native to tropical Asia and is commonly found in India and Sri Lanka. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and anti-fungal activities, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Adhatoda longifolia include its use in treating bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Adhatoda longifolia is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and can be consumed as a tea or added to herbal supplements to leverage its medicinal and health benefits.
